mnemonics: Mostly Odd People Always Choose Errors
Many Odd People Always Choose Errors
keyPoints: Comparison counting sort is a comparison-based sorting algorithm.
It works by counting the number of comparisons needed to sort the array.
The algorithm has a time complexity of O(n^2) but uses O(n) extra space.
It is not efficient for large datasets due to its high time complexity.
However, it is simple to implement and can be useful in certain situations.
